title: Your First 30 Days as a Rental Agent: Day-by-Day Plan
dek: Talk is cheap and most "new agent advice" is talk. Here's an actual first month, day by day, for an agent starting a rental business from zero.
heroAlt: Your First 30 Days as a Rental Agent: Day-by-Day Plan

blocks.html: Days 1-2: set up the Building Bible spreadsheet and list 50 luxury buildings in your market with basic data. Days 3-4: score them on turnover, agent policy, price point, and clustering; cut to your Power 25. Day 5: write your intro email and 30-second call script (templates are in my ebook). Days 6-7: polish your professional presence: bios, headshot, one link hub.

Days 8-10: contact all 25 buildings, email or call, using the script. Log every response in the Bible. Days 11-12: walk into your five nearest target buildings and introduce yourself in person. Days 13-14: set the recurring calendar rhythm: every building touched twice a month, forever. This is the machine; everything else feeds it.

blocks.text: Month one you're a name in a spreadsheet. Month six they call you first.

blocks.html: Days 15-17: announce what you do everywhere you exist online, and start the daily 15-minute engagement habit. Days 18-19: tell your personal network, specifically: "I help people find luxury rentals in [city], and I'd love to help anyone you know who's moving." Days 20-21: post your first content: one building, one lesson, one market observation.

Days 22-28: chase your first showings from any source: building overflow, network, online engagement. Run each one with the full choreography: early arrival, lobby greeting, agenda, lifestyle tour, application roadmap, and the ask. Days 29-30: review the numbers, thank every contact, and schedule month two.
Thirty days, zero dollars, and a real pipeline forming. The expanded version with every script lives in the $20 Luxury Leasing Playbook. Better agents win.

blocks.lead: The field manual, day by day.
